title: Length-Generalization Probe: Formal-Language Transducers
strategies: echo / memorizer: predict output == input; only the identity instances match and the longer test lengths never appear in training, so it stays at the ~0.1 baseline (the canonical length-generalization failure)
tiny fixed rule library: try identity / reversal / complement / parity, keep the first consistent with all training pairs; nails the easy tasks but is blind to sorting, shifts, counting, rotations

broad rule-library consistency search: enumerate many length-independent transducers (sort, double, dedup, rotate, suffix, pair-swap, caesar, plain-count, ...), pick the first consistent rule and extrapolate to the longer tests -- high but not perfect

true length-generalizer: infer the hidden modulus in the count-modulo trap and COMPOSE primitives (reverse-then-complement) rather than matching a single library entry, closing the headroom the library search leaves open
